companyContext: Synthetic fixtures for Stable leadership using Distillery v0 to capture text-only braindumps about StableChain, USD₮ payments, Stable Pay, gasless UX, ecosystem partners, protocol dependencies, compliance, GTM, and developer experience.
locatorConvention: Line ranges are inclusive over inputLines. Character offsets can be added later by the ingestion pipeline.

fixtures.title: Mainnet reliability before payments campaign
inputLines: Braindump after exec sync.
Do not scale the merchant payments campaign until we have a daily dashboard for p95 confirmation time, failed transfers, and RPC error rate.
The public message is instant USDt payments, but internally I only trust that claim once the reliability view is boring for two straight weeks.
Protocol owns the confirmation-time metric; DevRel owns RPC error reporting.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: The merchant payments campaign should not be scaled until Stable has a daily dashboard for p95 confirmation time, failed transfers, and RPC error rate.
Stable's public instant USDt payments message depends on reliability metrics being consistently stable for two straight weeks.
Protocol owns the confirmation-time metric and DevRel owns RPC error reporting.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer that the merchant payments campaign is approved to scale.
Do not invent actual p95, failed-transfer, or RPC error-rate values.

fixtures.title: Gas waiver pilot boundary
inputLines: Need to remember this from the product debate.
Gas waiver is the clearest UX unlock for first-time users who arrive with only USDT.
For v1, only sponsor merchant checkout and wallet activation flows; do not sponsor arbitrary DeFi interactions.
Governance approval for waiver addresses is a dependency before we promise this publicly.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: Gas waiver is viewed as a key UX unlock for first-time Stable users who arrive with only USDT.
The initial gas waiver scope is merchant checkout and wallet activation flows, not arbitrary DeFi interactions.
Publicly promising gas waiver depends on governance approval for waiver addresses.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer that gas waiver governance approval has already happened.
Do not expand the scope to all on-chain interactions.

fixtures.title: Stable Pay onboarding confusion
inputLines: Stable Pay notes from support review.
New users understand sending dollars, but they do not understand why the wallet asks them to choose a network.
Support wants copy that says Stable is the USDT payments network, not another trading app.
No decision yet on whether network selection should be hidden by default.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: New Stable Pay users understand sending dollars but are confused by wallet network selection.
Support wants copy that frames Stable as the USDT payments network rather than another trading app.
No decision has been made on whether Stable Pay should hide network selection by default.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer that network selection will be hidden.
Do not invent support ticket counts.

fixtures.title: Agentic payments narrative needs proof
inputLines: Agent payments narrative is promising, but we need proof before making it a flagship launch pillar.
The strongest wedge is agents paying for APIs in USDT without managing a volatile gas token.
Do not put this into Q3 commitments unless we have one design partner running an end-to-end demo.
DevRel should collect the demo requirements from infra partners.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: The agentic payments narrative needs proof before becoming a flagship launch pillar.
The proposed agentic payments wedge is agents paying for APIs in USDT without managing a volatile gas token.
Agentic payments should not enter Q3 commitments without one design partner running an end-to-end demo.
DevRel should collect agentic payments demo requirements from infrastructure partners.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer that agentic payments are a Q3 commitment.
Do not claim a design partner exists.

fixtures.title: Wallet partner asks for stable routing defaults
inputLines: Wallet partner braindump.
Partner can add Stable USDT support, but they want a default routing recommendation for users moving USDT from exchange balance to Stable wallet.
They also asked for a simple 'why Stable' explanation that avoids chain jargon.
BD says partner will not commit to homepage placement until routing and messaging are settled.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: A wallet partner's Stable USDT support depends on a default routing recommendation for users moving USDT from exchange balance to Stable wallet.
The wallet partner requested a simple 'why Stable' explanation that avoids chain jargon.
The wallet partner will not commit to homepage placement until routing and messaging are settled.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer homepage placement has been secured.
Do not invent a specific wallet partner name.

fixtures.title: Custody partner compliance export requirement
inputLines: Institutional custody partner feedback.
They like predictable USDT settlement, but onboarding is blocked until they can export address activity by client sub-account.
Compliance asked whether Stable can provide finality timestamp, transaction hash, counterparty address, and fee paid in USDT.
No one has committed engineering capacity for this export.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: An institutional custody partner likes predictable USDT settlement but is blocked until they can export address activity by client sub-account.
The custody partner's compliance export needs finality timestamp, transaction hash, counterparty address, and fee paid in USDT.
No engineering capacity has been committed for the custody compliance export.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer the export is approved.
Do not add KYC or sanctions requirements that are not stated.

fixtures.title: Merchant processor wants quote stability
inputLines: Merchant processor call.
They care less about DeFi compatibility and more about knowing the exact USDT cost of a payment before checkout confirmation.
They asked for a quote API or SDK helper that estimates fee and settlement status before the user confirms.
If we cannot provide that, they will keep Stable in research mode.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: A merchant processor prioritizes knowing the exact USDT cost of a payment before checkout confirmation over DeFi compatibility.
The merchant processor requested a quote API or SDK helper that estimates fee and settlement status before user confirmation.
Without quote support, the merchant processor will keep Stable in research mode.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer the merchant processor has committed to launch.
Do not invent API fields beyond fee and settlement status.

fixtures.title: Payout partner cash-out concern
inputLines: Global payouts partner notes.
They like sending USDT on Stable, but their customers judge the product by local cash-out reliability.
The partner asked for a launch-country matrix that pairs Stable transfer support with off-ramp availability.
Do not market this as global payroll until we can explain where cash-out is actually supported.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: A global payouts partner says customers judge Stable payouts by local cash-out reliability, not only USDT transfer success.
The payouts partner requested a launch-country matrix pairing Stable transfer support with off-ramp availability.
Stable should not market a global payroll use case until cash-out support by geography can be explained.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer global payroll is approved for launch.
Do not invent a country list.

fixtures.title: Chain setup docs friction
inputLines: DevRel braindump from onboarding sessions.
Developers keep asking for chain ID, RPC URL, explorer link, faucet, and a minimal transfer example in one place.
Current docs have the pieces, but the first app path feels scattered.
Docs owner should ship a single 'send your first USDT payment on Stable' guide before the next ecosystem push.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: Developers ask for Stable chain ID, RPC URL, explorer link, faucet, and a minimal transfer example in one place.
Stable's first-app documentation path feels scattered even though the pieces exist.
Docs owner should ship a single 'send your first USDT payment on Stable' guide before the next ecosystem push.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer the guide has shipped.
Do not invent the actual chain ID or RPC URL unless present in the source.

fixtures.title: Indexer latency blocks dashboards
inputLines: Ecosystem dashboard issue.
Teams building merchant analytics cannot show real-time payment status if indexer lag is more than 30 seconds.
Protocol says finality is not the blocker; indexer freshness and webhook reliability are.
Need owner for the webhook SLA before we invite more analytics partners.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: Merchant analytics teams cannot show real-time payment status if Stable indexer lag exceeds 30 seconds.
Merchant analytics readiness depends on indexer freshness and webhook reliability rather than finality alone.
Stable needs an owner for the webhook SLA before inviting more analytics partners.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer the webhook SLA owner.
Do not claim finality is a problem.

fixtures.title: Bridge liquidity before cross-chain claims
inputLines: Liquidity note.
Cross-chain USDT movement is part of the story, but the launch page should not imply every route is deep enough for institutional payments.
Before we pitch treasury teams, we need route-level limits, expected settlement time, and failure handling for bridge transfers.
Ecosystem owns partner inventory; Protocol owns failure-mode documentation.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: Stable's launch page should not imply every cross-chain USDT route is deep enough for institutional payments.
Pitching treasury teams depends on route-level limits, expected settlement time, and failure handling for bridge transfers.
Ecosystem owns bridge partner inventory and Protocol owns bridge failure-mode documentation.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer institutional bridge readiness.
Do not invent route limits.

fixtures.title: Session keys need risk limits
inputLines: Security review thought.
EIP-7702 session keys are useful for removing repeated signatures, but payment apps need strict spend limits and expiration windows.
Security does not want the first demo to allow open-ended delegated transfers.
Product can demo session keys only if the permissions screen clearly shows amount, merchant, and duration.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: Payment apps using EIP-7702 session keys need strict spend limits and expiration windows.
Security does not want the first session-key demo to allow open-ended delegated transfers.
A product demo of session keys requires a permissions screen that clearly shows amount, merchant, and duration.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer session keys are approved for production.
Do not remove the security constraints from the memory.

fixtures.title: Developer activation funnel
inputLines: Dev funnel snapshot from last week's cohort.
112 developers created a test wallet.
47 submitted at least one Stable transaction.
Only 19 completed the sample merchant checkout flow.
Biggest drop-off was after faucet funding, when users had to choose the right RPC endpoint.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: In last week's developer cohort, 112 developers created a test wallet.
In last week's developer cohort, 47 developers submitted at least one Stable transaction.
In last week's developer cohort, 19 developers completed the sample merchant checkout flow.
The largest developer activation drop-off occurred after faucet funding, when users had to choose the right RPC endpoint.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer this is a long-term trend.
Do not calculate percentages unless the evaluator explicitly allows derived metrics.

fixtures.title: Merchant pilot support burden
inputLines: Merchant pilot weekly readout.
Six pilot merchants processed live USDT payments on Stable this week.
Four merchants opened support threads about reconciliation exports.
Two merchants asked whether fees can be presented as a fixed USDT line item before checkout.
No merchant reported failed settlement in the pilot channel.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: Six pilot merchants processed live USDT payments on Stable this week.
Four pilot merchants opened support threads about reconciliation exports.
Two pilot merchants asked whether fees can be shown as a fixed USDT line item before checkout.
No merchant reported failed settlement in the pilot channel this week.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer all settlements succeeded outside the pilot channel.
Do not infer merchant pilots are ready for general availability.

fixtures.title: Stable Pay launch scope tension
inputLines: Old launch note from May: Stable Pay launch should include consumer wallet, merchant QR checkout, and rewards.
New July exec note: remove rewards from the first launch because rewards distract from the USDT payments story.
No final approval was made in the July note.
Marketing already drafted copy mentioning rewards.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: A May launch note said Stable Pay launch should include consumer wallet, merchant QR checkout, and rewards.
A July exec note proposed removing rewards from the first Stable Pay launch because rewards distract from the USDT payments story.
No final approval was made in the July note about removing rewards from the Stable Pay launch.
Marketing copy already mentions Stable Pay rewards despite unresolved launch scope.

expectedConflicts.description: May scope includes rewards while July exec note proposes removing rewards, but no final approval exists.

negativeExpectations: Do not mark rewards as removed from launch.
Do not ignore the marketing-copy risk.

fixtures.title: Gasless messaging versus compliance limits
inputLines: Marketing wants to say every Stable transaction can feel gasless.
Compliance note says sponsored transactions should initially exclude high-risk corridors and unknown merchant categories.
Product note says merchant checkout is the first gasless flow.
Leadership has not decided the external wording.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: Marketing wants to say every Stable transaction can feel gasless.
Compliance says sponsored transactions should initially exclude high-risk corridors and unknown merchant categories.
Product says merchant checkout is the first gasless flow.
Leadership has not decided the external wording for Stable's gasless messaging.

expectedConflicts.description: Broad gasless marketing language conflicts with compliance and product limits.

negativeExpectations: Do not approve the 'every transaction' messaging.
Do not drop the compliance exclusions.

fixtures.title: Country launch matrix needed
inputLines: Regulatory/commercial braindump.
Before any country-specific payout campaign, legal wants a matrix covering local cash-out partners, restricted merchant categories, and whether sponsored gas is allowed.
BD wants the matrix by corridor, not by continent, because partner readiness varies too much.
No country should be labeled launch-ready until legal and BD both sign off.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: Country-specific payout campaigns require a legal matrix covering local cash-out partners, restricted merchant categories, and whether sponsored gas is allowed.
BD wants payout readiness tracked by corridor rather than continent because partner readiness varies too much.
No country should be labeled launch-ready until legal and BD both sign off.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer any country is launch-ready.
Do not invent legal requirements beyond the stated matrix fields.

fixtures.title: Prioritize payments ecosystem over generic DeFi
inputLines: Ecosystem strategy note.
For the next partner sprint, prioritize wallets, ramps, custody, merchant processors, and payment analytics.
Do not spend BD bandwidth on generic yield farms unless they directly improve USDT payment utility.
The reason is positioning: Stable should be remembered as payment infrastructure, not another DeFi chain.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: The next Stable ecosystem partner sprint should prioritize wallets, ramps, custody, merchant processors, and payment analytics.
BD should not spend bandwidth on generic yield farms unless they directly improve USDT payment utility.
Stable's desired positioning is payment infrastructure rather than another DeFi chain.

negativeExpectations: Do not infer DeFi integrations are permanently rejected.
Do not create an initiative automatically.

fixtures.title: Vague executive slogan
inputLines: Random thought: Stable should feel like Stripe for global USDT.
Maybe the website needs more trust and less crypto energy.
No customer quotes, no decision, no owner yet.
Just parking the thought.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: There is an unvalidated positioning thought that Stable should feel more like global USDT payment infrastructure than a crypto-native product.

negativeExpectations: Do not store this as a decision.
Do not create website requirements.
Do not infer customer demand.

fixtures.title: Unsupported launch copy
inputLines: Draft launch copy idea.
Stable is the safest and fastest payment chain for every institution on earth.
Users will never worry about fees again.
Need proof before we say any of this; this is not approved copy.

expectedMemoryItems.statement: Draft Stable launch claims about being the safest and fastest payment chain and users never worrying about fees need proof and are not approved copy.

negativeExpectations: Do not store 'safest and fastest payment chain' as a fact.
Do not store 'users will never worry about fees again' as a proven user outcome.
Do not treat the copy as approved.
